original: Mario Bros. is first released by Nintendo in Japan as an arcade game featuring an Italian-American plumber
New study shows Andean condor, world's heaviest bird, can fly for 5 hours without flapping its wings (Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences)
India launches its Chandrayaan-3 spacecraft from the Sriharikota Space Centre on the country's third moon mission
American engineerRobert Goddardis granted the first patent for liquid-fueled rocket design
US Mariner IV, the first Mars probe, passes at 6,100 miles (9,800 km)
Scientists from the Large Hadron Collider announce the discovery of a new particle called the pentaquark
World Chimpanzee Day:Jane Goodallreleases "Dr. Jane's Dream, The Goodall Centre of Hope" in Tanzania, an immersive experience of pivotal moments from Dr. Goodall's life and the evolution of her work
First US national monument dedicated to a black American to preserve the boyhood home of agricultural scientist and inventorGeorge Washington Carverin Newton County, Missouri
Bastille Day - the French Revolution begins with the storming of the Bastille prison in Paris, now celebrated as France's National Day
Plans are announced for EPCOT Center at Disney World in Orlando, Florida
386BSD is released by Lynne Jolitz and William Jolitz, starting the open source operating system revolution.Linus Torvaldsreleases Linux soon afterwards.
Huge iceberg looming over Innaarsuit village, western Greenland prompts evacations in case it calves
Jay Forrester, Engineer, invented random-access magnetic core memory, born near Anselmo, Nebraska (d. 2016)
First public demonstration of ice made via refrigeration by Florida physician John Gorrie
116°F (47°C) recorded in Collegeville, Indiana (state record)
Hank Aaronhits his 500th home run off San Francisco Giant Mike McCormick
Houston Astro Eddie Mathews hits his 500th home run off SF GiantJuan Marichal
Houston Astro Don Wilson strikes out 18, beats Reds 6-1 at Crosley Field in Cincinnati
Rookie of the Year Award is renamed to honorJackie Robinson
Brian Selznick, American author and illustrator (The Invention of Hugo Cabret), born in East Brunswick Township, New Jersey
English mountaineer Edward Whymper leads the first expedition to climb the Matterhorn; four die on the descent
Claudia Kennedy, U.S. Army officer (first female three star general in US army), born in Frankfurt, Germany
Tatyana Kuznetsova, Soviet cosmonaut (youngest person selected for space training at 20 but never flew), born in Moscow, Soviet Union (d. 2018)
Lee Elder, American golfer (first African-American to play in the US Masters; 4 PGA Tour titles), born in Dallas, Texas (d. 2021)
Florence Bascom, American geologist and 1st woman hired by the US Geological Survey, born in Williamstown, Massachusetts (d. 1945)
Alvin J. Fellows of New Haven, Connecticut, patents the tape measure
American John T. Smith patents corkboard
Boston Red Sox pitcher Mel Parnell no-hits Chicago White Sox, 4-0 at Fenway Park, Boston
Plate umpire and catcher in a game are brothers; Bill Haller is umpire and Tom Haller is Detroit Tigers catcher; Kansas City Royals win, 1-0

ICC Men's Cricket ODI World Cup, Lord's, London: After the first super over in an ODI, which is also tied, New Zealand and England are separated by a boundary countback, awarding England their first title; Player of the Match:Ben Stokes(ENG) 84 not out (98)
Kenneth McArthur runs an Olympic record marathon in 2:36:54.8
Musical film adaptation of "Pink Floyd- The Wall," directed by Alan Parker, starringBob Geldof, and written byRoger Waters, premieres at the Empire, Leicester Square in London
Angélique Kidjo, Beninese singer-songwriter ("Djin, Djin"; "Eve"; "IFÉ"), and human rights activist, born in Ouidah, Republic of Dahomey
Gustav Klimt, Austrian Symbolist painter (The Kiss), born in Baumgarten, Austria-Hungary (d. 1918)
The New York Times erroneously declaresBabe Ruth's 700 home run record to stand for all time
Owl City (Adam Young) debuts major-label album Ocean Eyes, now a two-time certified Platinum album with "Fireflies" being certified Diamond (2023)
English Mercedes driverLewis Hamiltonwins a record sixth British Formula 1 Grand Prix at Silverstone, moving him one win clear ofJim ClarkandAlain Prost(5)
Columbia returns to Kennedy Space Center via Offutt AFB, Neb
USS Long Beach, first nuclear powered cruiser launched at Quincy, Massachusetts
Floods on the Japanese island of Kyushu kill 20 and displace 250,000
117°F (47°C) recorded in East St. Louis, Illinois (state record)
MLB Baltimore Oriole Bob Johnson's 6th straight hit as a pinch hitter
Jessye Normansings French national anthem, La Marseillaise at the Place de la Concorde, Paris, to celebrate 200th anniversary of the French Revolution
Leon Garfield, English children's author (Devil-in-the-Fog), born in Brighton, England (d. 1996)
Robin Olds, American fighter pilot, flying ace in WWII and the Vietnam War, born in Honolulu, Hawaii (d. 2007)
Akua Dixon, American jazz cellist, arranger, composer, and educator (Quartette Indigo), born in New York City
Kyle Gass, American guitarist and singer-songwriter (Tenacious D; Trainwreck), born in Walnut Creek, California
Evian Championship Women's Golf, Evian Resort GC: Japan's Ayaka Furue eagles the final hole to defeat Stephanie Kyriacou of Australia by 1 stroke for her first major title
Edith Renfrow Smith, American supercentenarian (110 years) and first African American woman to graduate from Grinnell College, born in Grinnell, Iowa
Johannes Peter Müller, German physiologist, made first distinction between motor and sensory nerves, born in Coblenz, First French Repu (d. 1858)
Andre-Louis Debierne, French chemist and physicist (discoverer of actinium element, worked with Marie Curie), born in Paris, France (d. 1949)
Andrew Cassell, British sailor and Paralympic gold medalist (1996), born in East Sussex, England (d. 2025)
George E. Lewis, American trombonist avant-garde jazz, electronic and contemporary classical composer, born in Chicago, Illinois
Bayeux Tapestry likely first goes on display to decorate the nave during the consecration of Notre-Dame of Bayeux Cathedral in Bayeux, Normandy
Mission San Antonio de Padua founded in California
French National Convention decrees "La Marseillaise" by Claude-Joseph Rouget de Lisle as France's national anthem
Commodore Perry requests trade relations with Japan
The Campanile in St Mark's Square, Venice collapses, also demolishing the loggetta (reconstructed 1912)
46 inches of rain begins to fall in Baguio, Philippines
13.2 inches (33.6 cm) of rainfall at Effingham, South Carolina (state record until 1999)
